records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3519 - Hisham ibn Salim narrated from Abu Basir, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
"Commander of the Faithful, peace be upon him, ruled regarding a person who mistreated his slave that the slave becomes free. There is no authority over him, and he is considered a 'sa'ibah' (free without obligation). He may choose to align himself with whomever he wishes, and if that person guarantees his liability, then he inherits from him."
records.arabicText: 3519 - وَ رَوَى هِشَامُ بْنُ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "قَضَى أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِيمَنْ نَكَّلَ بِمَمْلُوكِهِ أَنَّهُ حُرٌّ لاَ سَبِيلَ لَهُ عَلَيْهِ سَائِبَةٌ يَذْهَبُ فَيَتَوَلَّى إِلَى مَنْ أَحَبَّ فَإِذَا ضَمِنَ حَدَثَهُ فَهُوَ يَرِثُهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3519 - Hisham ibn Salim narrated from Abu Basir, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Commander of the Faithful, peace be upon him, ruled regarding a person who mistreated his slave that the slave becomes free. There is no authority over him, and he is considered a 'sa'ibah' (free without obligation). He may choose to align himself with whomever he wishes, and if that person guarantees his liability, then he inherits from him."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3520 - It is narrated regarding a woman who cut off the breast of her female slave;
"She (the slave) is free, and her mistress has no authority over her."
records.arabicText: 3520 - وَ رُوِيَ : فِي اِمْرَأَةٍ قَطَعَتْ ثَدْيَ وَلِيدَتِهَا "أَنَّهَا حُرَّةٌ لاَ سَبِيلَ لِمَوْلاَتِهَا عَلَيْهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3520 - It is narrated regarding a woman who cut off the breast of her female slave;
records.thaqalaynMatn: "She (the slave) is free, and her mistress has no authority over her."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3521 - It is narrated by Talhah bin Zayd, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), peace be upon him, regarding a man who freed part of his slave.
Imam (as) said: "He is completely free; there is no partner with Allah (swt), the Almighty."
records.arabicText: 3521 - وَ رَوَى طَلْحَةُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ أَعْتَقَ بَعْضَ مَمْلُوكِهِ قَالَ "هُوَ حُرٌّ كُلُّهُ لَيْسَ لِلَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ شَرِيكٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3521 - It is narrated by Talhah bin Zayd, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), peace be upon him, regarding a man who freed part of his slave.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "He is completely free; there is no partner with Allah (swt), the Almighty."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3522 - It is narrated by Al-Sakooni, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), peace be upon him, regarding a man who freed a pregnant bondwoman but excluded what was in her womb.
Imam (as) said: "The bondwoman is free, and what is in her womb is also free because what is in her womb is a part of her."
records.arabicText: 3522 - وَ رَوَى اَلسَّكُونِيُّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ أَعْتَقَ أَمَةً وَ هِيَ حُبْلَى فَاسْتَثْنَى مَا فِي بَطْنِهَا قَالَ "اَلْأَمَةُ حُرَّةٌ وَ مَا فِي بَطْنِهَا حُرٌّ لِأَنَّ مَا فِي بَطْنِهَا مِنْهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3522 - It is narrated by Al-Sakooni, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), peace be upon him, regarding a man who freed a pregnant bondwoman but excluded what was in her womb.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "The bondwoman is free, and what is in her womb is also free because what is in her womb is a part of her."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3523 - It is narrated from Saif bin Amira, who said:
"I asked Abu Abdullah (as), 'Is it permissible for a Muslim to free a slave who is a polytheist?'"
Imam (as) said: "No."
records.arabicText: 3523 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ سَيْفِ بْنِ عَمِيرَةَ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَ يَجُوزُ لِلْمُسْلِمِ أَنْ يُعْتِقَ مَمْلُوكاً مُشْرِكاً قَالَ "لاَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3523 - It is narrated from Saif bin Amira, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked Abu Abdullah (as), 'Is it permissible for a Muslim to free a slave who is a polytheist?'"
Imam (as) said: "No."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3524 - It is narrated by Abu Al-Bakhtari from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
"It is not permissible in emancipation (freeing a slave) for the blind, the one-eyed, or the crippled. However, it is permissible for the one with a paralyzed hand or the lame."
records.arabicText: 3524 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو اَلْبَخْتَرِيِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "لاَ يَجُوزُ فِي اَلْعَتَاقِ اَلْأَعْمَى وَ اَلْأَعْوَرُ وَ اَلْمُقْعَدُ وَ يَجُوزُ اَلْأَشَلُّ وَ اَلْأَعْرَجُ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3524 - It is narrated by Abu Al-Bakhtari from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is not permissible in emancipation (freeing a slave) for the blind, the one-eyed, or the crippled. However, it is permissible for the one with a paralyzed hand or the lame."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3525 - It is narrated from Ali bin Ja'far from his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as), who said:
"I asked him about a man who is obligated to free a slave and intends to emancipate one. Which is better-to free an old man or a young, healthy man?"
Imam (as) replied: 'Free the one who is more self-sufficient. The old man is better than the young, healthy (strong) man.' "
records.arabicText:
3525 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ أَخِيهِ مُوسَى بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ :
سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ عَلَيْهِ عِتْقُ رَقَبَةٍ فَأَرَادَ أَنْ يُعْتِقَ نَسَمَةً أَيُّهُمَا أَفْضَلُ أَنْ يُعْتِقَ شَيْخاً كَبِيراً أَوْ شَابّاً أَجْرَدَ قَالَ "أَعْتَقَ مَنْ أَغْنَى نَفْسَهُ اَلشَّيْخُ اَلْكَبِيرُ أَفْضَلُ مِنَ اَلشَّابِّ اَلْأَجْرَدِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3525 - It is narrated from Ali bin Ja'far from his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked him about a man who is obligated to free a slave and intends to emancipate one. Which is better-to free an old man or a young, healthy man?"
Imam (as) replied: 'Free the one who is more self-sufficient. The old man is better than the young, healthy (strong) man.' "

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3526 - It is narrated from Ahmad bin Hilal, who said:
"I wrote to Abu al-Hasan (as), stating that I was obligated to free a slave, but my slave had escaped, and I did not know where he was. Would his emancipation suffice for fulfilling my obligation?"
Imam (as) wrote back: 'Yes.' "
records.arabicText: 3526 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ هِلاَلٍ قَالَ : كَتَبْتُ إِلَى أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ كَانَ عَلَيَّ عِتْقُ رَقَبَةٍ فَهَرَبَ لِي مَمْلُوكٌ لَسْتُ أَعْلَمُ أَيْنَ هُوَ أَ يُجْزِينِي عِتْقُهُ فَكَتَبَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "نَعَمْ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3526 - It is narrated from Ahmad bin Hilal, who said:
"I wrote to Abu al-Hasan (as), stating that I was obligated to free a slave, but my slave had escaped, and I did not know where he was. Would his emancipation suffice for fulfilling my obligation?"
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) wrote back: 'Yes.' "

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3527 - It is narrated from Abu Hashim al-Ja'fari, who said:
"I asked Abu al-Hasan (as), about a man who had a runaway slave. Is it permissible for him to free the slave as an expiation for zihar?"
Imam (as) replied: 'There is no harm in it, as long as he has not come to know of the slave's death.'"
records.arabicText: 3527 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي هَاشِمٍ اَلْجَعْفَرِيِّ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ لَهُ مَمْلُوكٌ قَدْ أَبَقَ مِنْهُ يَجُوزُ أَنْ يُعْتِقَهُ فِي كَفَّارَةِ اَلظِّهَارِ قَالَ "لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ مَا لَمْ يَعْرِفْ مِنْهُ مَوْتاً".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3527 - It is narrated from Abu Hashim al-Ja'fari, who said:
"I asked Abu al-Hasan (as), about a man who had a runaway slave. Is it permissible for him to free the slave as an expiation for zihar?"
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: 'There is no harm in it, as long as he has not come to know of the slave's death.'"

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on Compensation for judges' Errors
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3231 - It is narrated from Al-Asbagh ibn Nubatah that he said:
Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ruled that any error made by judges concerning bloodshed or amputation is to be compensated from the public treasury of the Muslims.
records.arabicText: 3231 - رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْأَصْبَغِ بْنِ نُبَاتَةَ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : قَضَى أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّ مَا أَخْطَأَتِ اَلْقُضَاةُ فِي دَمٍ أَوْ قَطْعٍ فَهُوَ عَلَى بَيْتِ مَالِ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3231 - It is narrated from Al-Asbagh ibn Nubatah that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ruled that any error made by judges concerning bloodshed or amputation is to be compensated from the public treasury of the Muslims.

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3456 - Ishaq ibn Ammar asked Abu Ibrahim (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim) (as), about a man who frees his slave as a delayed emancipation (to take effect after his death) and then becomes in need of the slave's price.
Imam (as) said: "He may sell him."
I asked: "What if he is not in need of the price?"
Imam (as) said: "If the slave agrees, then there is no harm."
records.arabicText:
3456 - سَأَلَ إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ أَبَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ :
عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُعْتِقُ مَمْلُوكَهُ عَنْ دُبُرٍ ثُمَّ يَحْتَاجُ إِلَى ثَمَنِهِ قَالَ "يَبِيعُهُ" قَالَ قُلْتُ فَإِنْ كَانَ لَهُ عَنْ ثَمَنِهِ غِنًى قَالَ "إِذَا رَضِيَ اَلْمَمْلُوكُ فَلاَ بَأْسَ".

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3457 - Jamil narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
I asked him about a mudabbar (a slave promised freedom after the owner's death).
Imam (as) said: "If his owner needs the price and the slave agrees, then there is no harm in selling him."
records.arabicText: 3457 - وَ رَوَى جَمِيلٌ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْمُدَبَّرِ أَ يُبَاعُ قَالَ "إِنِ اِحْتَاجَ صَاحِبُهُ إِلَى ثَمَنِهِ وَ رَضِيَ اَلْمَمْلُوكُ فَلاَ بَأْسَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3457 - Jamil narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
I asked him about a mudabbar (a slave promised freedom after the owner's death).
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If his owner needs the price and the slave agrees, then there is no harm in selling him."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3458 - It is narrated by al-Ala', from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from one of the Imams (as), peace be upon them, regarding a man who frees his male or female slave as a mudabbar (to be freed after his death) and then becomes in need of the slave's price.
Imam (as) said: "No, he may not sell the slave-unless he stipulates to the buyer that the slave must be freed at the time of his death."
records.arabicText: 3458 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْعَلاَءِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يُعْتِقُ غُلاَمَهُ أَوْ جَارِيَتَهُ عَنْ دُبُرٍ مِنْهُ ثُمَّ يَحْتَاجُ إِلَى ثَمَنِهِ أَ يَبِيعُهُ قَالَ "لاَ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَشْتَرِطَ عَلَى اَلَّذِي يَبِيعُهُ إِيَّاهُ أَنْ يُعْتِقَهُ عِنْدَ مَوْتِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3458 - It is narrated by al-Ala', from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from one of the Imams (as), peace be upon them, regarding a man who frees his male or female slave as a mudabbar (to be freed after his death) and then becomes in need of the slave's price.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "No, he may not sell the slave-unless he stipulates to the buyer that the slave must be freed at the time of his death."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3459 - Abu Ibrahim (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as)) was asked about a woman who made her bondwoman mudabbar (to be freed after her death). The bondwoman later gave birth to a precious daughter, and it was unclear whether the child was also mudabbar like her mother or not.
Imam (as) said: "When the pregnancy occurred, was it before or after the tadbir (declaration of delayed emancipation)?"
I said: "May I be your ransom, I do not know. Please answer regarding both situations."
Imam (as) said: "If the bondwoman was pregnant before the tadbir and her pregnancy was not mentioned during the declaration, then the bondwoman is mudabbar, but what is in her womb remains in bondage (not mudabbar).
However, if the tadbir occurred before the pregnancy and then she became pregnant, the child is mudabbar along with her, because the pregnancy occurred after the tadbir."
records.arabicText: 3459 - وَ سُئِلَ أَبُو إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اِمْرَأَةٍ دَبَّرَتْ جَارِيَةً لَهَا فَوَلَدَتِ اَلْجَارِيَةُ جَارِيَةً نَفِيسَةً فَلَمْ يُدْرَ أَ مُدَبَّرَةٌ هِيَ مِثْلُ أُمِّهَا أَمْ لاَ فَقَالَ "مَتَى كَانَ اَلْحَمْلُ كَانَ وَ هِيَ مُدَبَّرَةٌ أَوْ قَبْلَ اَلتَّدْبِيرِ" قُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ لاَ أَدْرِي أَجِبْنِي فِيهِمَا جَمِيعاً فَقَالَ "إِنْ كَانَتِ اَلْجَارِيَةُ حُبْلَى قَبْلَ اَلتَّدْبِيرِ وَ لَمْ يَذْكُرْ مَا فِي بَطْنِهَا فَالْجَارِيَةُ مُدَبَّرَةٌ وَ مَا فِي بَطْنِهَا رِقٌّ وَ إِنْ كَانَ اَلتَّدْبِيرُ قَبْلَ اَلْحَمْلِ ثُمَّ حَدَثَ اَلْحَمْلُ فَالْوَلَدُ مُدَبَّرٌ مَعَ أُمِّهِ لِأَنَّ اَلْحَمْلَ إِنَّمَا حَدَثَ بَعْدَ اَلتَّدْبِيرِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3459 - Abu Ibrahim (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as)) was asked about a woman who made her bondwoman mudabbar (to be freed after her death). The bondwoman later gave birth to a precious daughter, and it was unclear whether the child was also mudabbar like her mother or not.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"When the pregnancy occurred, was it before or after the tadbir (declaration of delayed emancipation)?"
I said: "May I be your ransom, I do not know. Please answer regarding both situations."
Imam (as) said: "If the bondwoman was pregnant before the tadbir and her pregnancy was not mentioned during the declaration, then the bondwoman is mudabbar, but what is in her womb remains in bondage (not mudabbar).
However, if the tadbir occurred before the pregnancy and then she became pregnant, the child is mudabbar along with her, because the pregnancy occurred after the tadbir."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3460 - Al-Hasan ibn Ali al-Washsha' asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about a man who made his bondwoman mudabbar (to be freed after his death) while she was pregnant.
Imam (as) said: "If he knew about the pregnancy of the bondwoman, then what is in her womb has the same status as her (i.e., it is also mudabbar). But if he did not know, then what is in her womb remains in bondage (not mudabbar)."
Al-Hasan ibn Ali al-Washsha' further asked him about a man who made his slave mudabbar while he was financially well-off, but later he became needy.
Imam (as) said: "Yes, if he becomes in need, it is permissible for him to sell the slave."
records.arabicText: 3460 - وَ سَأَلَ اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ اَلْوَشَّاءُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ رَجُلٍ دَبَّرَ جَارِيَةً وَ هِيَ حُبْلَى فَقَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ عَلِمَ بِحَبَلِ اَلْجَارِيَةِ فَمَا فِي بَطْنِهَا بِمَنْزِلَتِهَا وَ إِنْ كَانَ لَمْ يَعْلَمْ فَمَا فِي بَطْنِهَا رِقٌّ " قَالَ وَ سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُدَبِّرُ اَلْمَمْلُوكَ وَ هُوَ حَسَنُ اَلْحَالِ ثُمَّ يَحْتَاجُ أَ يَجُوزُ لَهُ أَنْ يَبِيعَهُ قَالَ "نَعَمْ إِذَا اِحْتَاجَ إِلَى ذَلِكَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3460 - Al-Hasan ibn Ali al-Washsha' asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about a man who made his bondwoman mudabbar (to be freed after his death) while she was pregnant.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"If he knew about the pregnancy of the bondwoman, then what is in her womb has the same status as her (i.e., it is also mudabbar). But if he did not know, then what is in her womb remains in bondage (not mudabbar)."
Al-Hasan ibn Ali al-Washsha' further asked him about a man who made his slave mudabbar while he was financially well-off, but later he became needy.
Imam (as) said: "Yes, if he becomes in need, it is permissible for him to sell the slave."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3461 - It is narrated from al-Ala', from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from one of the Imams (as), peace be upon them, who said:
"A mudabbar (a slave promised freedom after the owner's death) is considered to be from the one-third (of the estate). A man has the right to retract from his bequest regarding this one-third, whether he made the will during his health or illness."
records.arabicText: 3461 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْعَلاَءِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "اَلْمُدَبَّرُ مِنَ اَلثُّلُثِ وَ لِلرَّجُلِ أَنْ يَرْجِعَ فِي ثُلُثِهِ إِنْ كَانَ أَوْصَى فِي صِحَّةٍ أَوْ مَرَضٍ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3461 - It is narrated from al-Ala', from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from one of the Imams (as), peace be upon them, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A mudabbar (a slave promised freedom after the owner's death) is considered to be from the one-third (of the estate). A man has the right to retract from his bequest regarding this one-third, whether he made the will during his health or illness."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3462 - Aban narrated from Abu Maryam, from Abu Abdullah (as), who was asked about a man who made his bondwoman mudabbar (to be freed after his death).
Imam (as) was asked: "Can he have relations with her if he wishes, or marry her off, or sell her service during his lifetime?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, he may do any of these if he wishes."
records.arabicText: 3462 - وَ رَوَى أَبَانٌ عَنْ أَبِي مَرْيَمَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سُئِلَ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُعْتِقُ جَارِيَتَهُ عَنْ دُبُرٍ أَ يَطَؤُهَا إِنْ شَاءَ أَوْ يُنْكِحُهَا أَوْ يَبِيعُ خِدْمَتَهَا حَيَاتَهُ قَالَ "نَعَمْ أَيَّ ذَلِكَ شَاءَ فَعَلَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3462 - Aban narrated from Abu Maryam, from Abu Abdullah (as), who was asked about a man who made his bondwoman mudabbar (to be freed after his death).
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) was asked: "Can he have relations with her if he wishes, or marry her off, or sell her service during his lifetime?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, he may do any of these if he wishes."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3463 - Asim narrated from Abu Basir, who said:
I asked him about a male or female slave who had been made mudabbar (to be freed after the master's death).
Imam (as) said: "The master may enter into a mukataba (a contract of emancipation in exchange for payment) with the slave if he wishes. However, he cannot sell the slave unless the slave agrees to be sold for the duration of the master's life. The master is also entitled to take any wealth the slave possesses, if the slave has wealth."
records.arabicText: 3463 - وَ رَوَى عَاصِمٌ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْعَبْدِ وَ اَلْأَمَةِ يُعْتَقَانِ عَنْ دُبُرٍ فَقَالَ "لِمَوْلاَهُ أَنْ يُكَاتِبَهُ إِنْ شَاءَ وَ لَيْسَ لَهُ أَنْ يَبِيعَهُ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَشَاءَ اَلْعَبْدُ أَنْ يَبِيعَهُ مُدَّةَ حَيَاتِهِ وَ لَهُ أَنْ يَأْخُذَ مَالَهُ إِنْ كَانَ لَهُ مَالٌ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3463 - Asim narrated from Abu Basir, who said:
I asked him about a male or female slave who had been made mudabbar (to be freed after the master's death).
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The master may enter into a mukataba (a contract of emancipation in exchange for payment) with the slave if he wishes. However, he cannot sell the slave unless the slave agrees to be sold for the duration of the master's life. The master is also entitled to take any wealth the slave possesses, if the slave has wealth."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3464 - Abdullah ibn Sinan asked him about a woman who freed one-third of her servant at the time of her death.
He asked: "Are her heirs required to enter into a mukataba (a contract of emancipation in exchange for payment) with the servant if they wish, or can they refuse?"
Imam (as) said: "No, but the servant owns one-third of herself, and the heirs own two-thirds. The heirs may use her services in proportion to their share, and she has freedom over herself in proportion to the part that was freed."
records.arabicText: 3464 - وَ سَأَلَهُ عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ : عَنِ اِمْرَأَةٍ أَعْتَقَتْ ثُلُثَ خَادِمِهَا عِنْدَ مَوْتِهَا أَ عَلَى أَهْلِهَا أَنْ يُكَاتِبُوهَا إِنْ شَاءُوا وَ إِنْ أَبَوْا قَالَ "لاَ وَ لَكِنْ لَهَا مِنْ نَفْسِهَا ثُلُثُهَا وَ لِلْوَارِثِ ثُلُثَاهَا يَسْتَخْدِمُهَا بِحِسَابِ اَلَّذِي لَهُ مِنْهَا وَ يَكُونُ لَهَا مِنْ نَفْسِهَا بِحِسَابِ مَا أُعْتِقَ مِنْهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3464 - Abdullah ibn Sinan asked him about a woman who freed one-third of her servant at the time of her death.
He asked:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Are her heirs required to enter into a mukataba (a contract of emancipation in exchange for payment) with the servant if they wish, or can they refuse?"
Imam (as) said: "No, but the servant owns one-third of herself, and the heirs own two-thirds. The heirs may use her services in proportion to their share, and she has freedom over herself in proportion to the part that was freed."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3465 - Aban narrated from Abd al-Rahman, who said:
I asked him about a man who said to his slave, "If something happens to me, you are free." Meanwhile, the man is obligated to free a slave as an expiation for breaking an oath or zihar (a form of prohibited declaration regarding one's wife).
I asked: "Can he use this same slave, whom he had promised freedom upon his death, to fulfill the requirement of freeing a slave for the expiation?"
Imam (as) said: "No, it is not permissible to use the slave designated for that purpose."
records.arabicText: 3465 - وَ رَوَى أَبَانٌ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ قَالَ لِعَبْدِهِ إِنْ حَدَثَ بِي حَدَثٌ فَهُوَ حُرٌّ وَ عَلَى اَلرَّجُلِ تَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ فِي كَفَّارَةِ يَمِينٍ أَوْ ظِهَارٍ أَ لَهُ أَنْ يُعْتِقَ عَبْدَهُ اَلَّذِي جَعَلَ لَهُ اَلْعِتْقَ إِنْ حَدَثَ بِهِ حَدَثٌ فِي كَفَّارَةِ تِلْكَ اَلْيَمِينِ قَالَ "لاَ يَجُوزُ اَلَّذِي يَجْعَلُ لَهُ فِي ذَلِكَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3465 - Aban narrated from Abd al-Rahman, who said:
I asked him about a man who said to his slave,
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"If something happens to me, you are free." Meanwhile, the man is obligated to free a slave as an expiation for breaking an oath or zihar (a form of prohibited declaration regarding one's wife).
I asked: "Can he use this same slave, whom he had promised freedom upon his death, to fulfill the requirement of freeing a slave for the expiation?"
Imam (as) said: "No, it is not permissible to use the slave designated for that purpose."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3466 - Wuhayb ibn Hafs narrated from Abu Basir, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as), about a man who made his slave mudabbar (to be freed after his death) while he was in debt, intending to escape from paying the debt.
Imam (as) said: "There is no valid tadbir (declaration of delayed emancipation) in this case. However, if he made the tadbir while he was in good health and free from any financial distress, then the creditors have no claim over the slave."
records.arabicText: 3466 - وَ رَوَى وُهَيْبُ بْنُ حَفْصٍ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ دَبَّرَ غُلاَمَهُ وَ عَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ فِرَاراً مِنَ اَلدَّيْنِ قَالَ "لاَ تَدْبِيرَ لَهُ وَ إِنْ كَانَ دَبَّرَهُ فِي صِحَّةٍ مِنْهُ وَ سَلاَمَةٍ فَلاَ سَبِيلَ لِلدُّيَّانِ عَلَيْهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3466 - Wuhayb ibn Hafs narrated from Abu Basir, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as), about a man who made his slave mudabbar (to be freed after his death) while he was in debt, intending to escape from paying the debt.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "There is no valid tadbir (declaration of delayed emancipation) in this case. However, if he made the tadbir while he was in good health and free from any financial distress, then the creditors have no claim over the slave."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3467 - Ibn Mahbub narrated from Ali ibn Ri'ab, from Burayd ibn Mu'awiyah, who said:
I asked Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), about a man who made his wealthy slave mudabbar (to be freed after his death). The mudabbar, with the permission of his master, bought a bondwoman who bore him children. Then the mudabbar died before his master.
Imam (as) said: "I believe that all the property, possessions, or estates left by the mudabbar belong to the one who made him mudabbar. As for the mother of his children, she remains a slave to the one who made him mudabbar. However, I see that her children take the same status as their father-they are mudabbar. Therefore, when the one who made their father mudabbar dies, they will be freed."
records.arabicText: 3467 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ رِئَابٍ عَنْ بُرَيْدِ بْنِ مُعَاوِيَةَ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ دَبَّرَ مَمْلُوكاً لَهُ تَاجِراً مُوسِراً فَاشْتَرَى اَلْمُدَبَّرُ جَارِيَةً بِأَمْرِ مَوْلاَهُ فَوَلَدَتْ مِنْهُ أَوْلاَداً ثُمَّ إِنَّ اَلْمُدَبَّرَ مَاتَ قَبْلَ سَيِّدِهِ فَقَالَ "أَرَى أَنَّ جَمِيعَ مَا تَرَكَ اَلْمُدَبَّرُ مِنْ مَتَاعٍ أَوْ ضِيَاعٍ فَهُوَ لِلَّذِي دَبَّرَهُ وَ أَرَى أَنَّ أُمَّ وَلَدِهِ رِقٌّ لِلَّذِي دَبَّرَهُ وَ أَرَى أَنَّ وُلْدَهَا مُدَبَّرُونَ كَهَيْئَةِ أَبِيهِمْ فَإِذَا مَاتَ اَلَّذِي دَبَّرَ أَبَاهُمْ فَهُمْ أَحْرَارٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3467 - Ibn Mahbub narrated from Ali ibn Ri'ab, from Burayd ibn Mu'awiyah, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), about a man who made his wealthy slave mudabbar (to be freed after his death). The mudabbar, with the permission of his master, bought a bondwoman who bore him children. Then the mudabbar died before his master.
Imam (as) said: "I believe that all the property, possessions, or estates left by the mudabbar belong to the one who made him mudabbar. As for the mother of his children, she remains a slave to the one who made him mudabbar. However, I see that her children take the same status as their father-they are mudabbar. Therefore, when the one who made their father mudabbar dies, they will be freed."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3468 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
"A mudabbar (a slave promised freedom after the owner's death) is counted as part of the one-third (of the estate). As for any offense committed by the mudabbar, the mukatab (a slave under a contract of emancipation), or the umm al-walad (a bondwoman who bore her master a child), the master is liable for their offenses."
records.arabicText: 3468 - وَ قَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "اَلْمُعْتَقُ عَنْ دُبُرٍ هُوَ مِنَ اَلثُّلُثِ وَ مَا جَنَى هُوَ وَ اَلْمُكَاتَبُ وَ أُمُّ اَلْوَلَدِ فَالْمَوْلَى ضَامِنٌ لِجِنَايَتِهِمْ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3468 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A mudabbar (a slave promised freedom after the owner's death) is counted as part of the one-third (of the estate). As for any offense committed by the mudabbar, the mukatab (a slave under a contract of emancipation), or the umm al-walad (a bondwoman who bore her master a child), the master is liable for their offenses."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on the Contract of Manumission (mukataba)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3469 - Muhammad ibn Sinan narrated from al-Ala' ibn al-Fudayl, from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding the saying of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic: "Then write [a contract of emancipation] for them if you know any good in them" (Surah An-Nur 24:33).
Imam (as) said: "If you know that they possess wealth."
I asked about the verse: 'And give them from the wealth of Allah which He has given you,' (Surah An-Nur 24:33).
Imam (as) said: "You reduce for them from their installments what you did not initially intend to reduce, and do not increase it beyond what you originally intended."
I asked: "How much?"
Imam (as) said: "Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), reduced one thousand out of six thousand for one of his slaves."
records.arabicText: 3469 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سِنَانٍ عَنِ اَلْعَلاَءِ بْنِ اَلْفُضَيْلِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ: ۝ فَكٰاتِبُوهُمْ إِنْ عَلِمْتُمْ فِيهِمْ خَيْراً ۝ قَالَ "إِنْ عَلِمْتُمْ لَهُمْ مَالاً " قَالَ قُلْتُ ۝ وَ آتُوهُمْ مِنْ مٰالِ اَللّٰهِ اَلَّذِي آتٰاكُمْ ۝ قَالَ "تَضَعُ عَنْهُ مِنْ نُجُومِهِ اَلَّتِي لَمْ تَكُنْ تُرِيدُ أَنْ تَنْقُصَهُ مِنْهَا شَيْئاً وَ لاَ تَزِيدُهُ فَوْقَ مَا فِي نَفْسِكَ" فَقُلْتُ كَمْ قَالَ "وَضَعَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ لِمَمْلُوكٍ لَهُ أَلْفاً مِنْ سِتَّةِ آلاَفٍ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3469 - Muhammad ibn Sinan narrated from al-Ala' ibn al-Fudayl, from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding the saying of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic: "Then write [a contract of emancipation] for them if you know any good in them" (Surah An-Nur 24:33).
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"If you know that they possess wealth."
I asked about the verse: 'And give them from the wealth of Allah which He has given you,' (Surah An-Nur 24:33).
Imam (as) said: "You reduce for them from their installments what you did not initially intend to reduce, and do not increase it beyond what you originally intended."
I asked: "How much?"
Imam (as) said: "Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), reduced one thousand out of six thousand for one of his slaves."

records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on the Contract of Manumission (mukataba)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3470 - Amr ibn Shimr narrated from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
I asked him about a mukatab (a slave under a contract of emancipation) who had a condition stipulated that if he failed to make payments, he would return to slavery. What if he failed before paying anything?
Imam (as) said: "He does not return to slavery until three years have passed. Whatever portion he has already paid counts toward his emancipation. If he has made partial payments, they cannot return him to slavery."
records.arabicText: 3470 - وَ رَوَى عَمْرُو بْنُ شِمْرٍ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْمُكَاتَبِ يُشْتَرَطُ عَلَيْهِ إِنْ عَجَزَ فَهُوَ رَدٌّ فِي اَلرِّقِّ فَعَجَزَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُؤَدِّيَ شَيْئاً قَالَ "لاَ يُرَدُّ فِي اَلرِّقِّ حَتَّى يَمْضِيَ لَهُ ثَلاَثُ سِنِينَ وَ يُعْتَقُ مِنْهُ مِقْدَارُ مَا أَدَّى صَدْراً فَإِذَا أَدَّى صَدْراً فَلَيْسَ لَهُمْ أَنْ يَرُدُّوهُ فِي اَلرِّقِّ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3470 - Amr ibn Shimr narrated from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
I asked him about a mukatab (a slave under a contract of emancipation) who had a condition stipulated that if he failed to make payments, he would return to slavery. What if he failed before paying anything?
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He does not return to slavery until three years have passed. Whatever portion he has already paid counts toward his emancipation. If he has made partial payments, they cannot return him to slavery."
